 {"id":"bd-51fm","title":"Implement repo_preflight_check()","description":"# Repository Preflight Checks

## Parent Epic: bd-cpxq (Preflight Safety Checks)

## Purpose
Validate repo is in safe state before invoking agent.

## Implementation

```bash
repo_preflight_check() {
    local repo_path="$1"
    PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON=""

    # Is it a git repo?
    if ! git -C "$repo_path" rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree &>/dev/null; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="not_a_git_repo"
        return 1
    fi

    # Git identity configured? (ADDED: prevents commit failures)
    if [[ -z "$(git -C "$repo_path" config user.email 2>/dev/null)" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="git_email_not_configured"
        return 1
    fi
    if [[ -z "$(git -C "$repo_path" config user.name 2>/dev/null)" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="git_name_not_configured"
        return 1
    fi

    # Shallow clone? (ADDED: some operations may fail)
    if [[ -f "$repo_path/.git/shallow" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="shallow_clone"
        return 1
    fi

    # Dirty submodules? (ADDED: complex state)
    if git -C "$repo_path" submodule status 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^+'; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="dirty_submodules"
        return 1
    fi

    # Rebase in progress?
    if [[ -d "$repo_path/.git/rebase-apply" ]] || [[ -d "$repo_path/.git/rebase-merge" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="rebase_in_progress"
        return 1
    fi

    # Merge in progress?
    if [[ -f "$repo_path/.git/MERGE_HEAD" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="merge_in_progress"
        return 1
    fi

    # Cherry-pick in progress?
    if [[ -f "$repo_path/.git/CHERRY_PICK_HEAD" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="cherry_pick_in_progress"
        return 1
    fi

    # Detached HEAD?
    local branch
    branch=$(git -C "$repo_path" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
    if [[ -z "$branch" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="detached_HEAD"
        return 1
    fi

    # Has upstream?
    local upstream
    upstream=$(git -C "$repo_path" rev-parse --abbrev-ref "@{u}" 2>/dev/null)
    if [[ -z "$upstream" ]] && [[ "${AGENT_SWEEP_PUSH_STRATEGY:-push}" != "none" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="no_upstream_branch"
        return 1
    fi

    # Diverged from upstream?
    if [[ -n "$upstream" ]]; then
        local ahead behind
        read -r ahead behind < <(git -C "$repo_path" rev-list --left-right --count HEAD...@{u} 2>/dev/null)
        if [[ "$ahead" -gt 0 ]] && [[ "$behind" -gt 0 ]]; then
            PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="diverged_from_upstream"
            return 1
        fi
    fi

    # Unmerged paths?
    if git -C "$repo_path" ls-files --unmerged 2>/dev/null | grep -q .; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="unmerged_paths"
        return 1
    fi

    # git diff --check clean?
    if ! git -C "$repo_path" diff --check &>/dev/null; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="diff_check_failed"
        return 1
    fi

    # Too many untracked files?
    local untracked_count
    untracked_count=$(git -C "$repo_path" ls-files --others --exclude-standard 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
    if [[ "$untracked_count" -gt "${AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_UNTRACKED:-1000}" ]]; then
        PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON="too_many_untracked_files"
        return 1
    fi

    return 0
}
```

## Skip Reason to User Action Mapping

| Reason | User Action |
|--------|-------------|
| git_email_not_configured | Run: git config user.email "you@example.com" |
| git_name_not_configured | Run: git config user.name "Your Name" |
| shallow_clone | Run: git fetch --unshallow |
| dirty_submodules | Fix submodule state first |
| rebase_in_progress | Complete or abort rebase |
| merge_in_progress | Complete or abort merge |
| detached_HEAD | Switch to a branch |
| no_upstream_branch | Set upstream tracking branch |
| diverged_from_upstream | Pull and rebase first |
| too_many_untracked_files | Review .gitignore, clean untracked |

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 13 preflight conditions checked
- [ ] Skip reasons are human-readable
- [ ] User action mapping covers all skip reasons
- [ ] Tests cover all skip scenarios

 {"id":"bd-52ah","title":"Update install.sh with ntm integration","description":"# Installer Integration for ntm

## Parent Epic: bd-3ov0 (Installer Integration)

## Changes to install.sh

### 1. Add ntm Detection
```bash
check_ntm_installed() {
    command -v ntm &>/dev/null
}

get_ntm_version() {
    ntm --version 2>/dev/null | head -1
}
```

### 2. Add Interactive Prompt
```bash
prompt_ntm_install() {
    [[ "${RU_NON_INTERACTIVE:-}" == "1" ]] && return 0
    [[ "${RU_INSTALL_NTM:-}" == "yes" ]] && return 0
    [[ "${RU_INSTALL_NTM:-}" == "no" ]] && return 1
    
    cat << EOF
╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│          Enable ntm integration?                            │
│                                                             │
│   Enables: ru agent-sweep                                   │
│   Provides: AI-assisted commit and release automation       │
│                                                             │
│   [Y] Yes (recommended)                                     │
│   [n] No, skip for now                                      │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
EOF
    read -r response
    [[ "$response" =~ ^[yY]$ ]] || [[ -z "$response" ]]
}
```

### 3. Add ntm Installation
```bash
install_ntm() {
    if check_ntm_installed; then
        log_info "ntm already installed: $(get_ntm_version)"
        return 0
    fi
    
    log_step "Installing ntm..."
    curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/main/install.sh | bash
}
```

### 4. Integration Flow
After ru install:
1. Check tmux (warn if missing)
2. Prompt for ntm (respects env vars)
3. Install ntm if requested
4. Verify installation

### Environment Variables
- RU_INSTALL_NTM=yes: Auto-install ntm
- RU_INSTALL_NTM=no: Skip ntm
- RU_NON_INTERACTIVE=1: Install everything","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:56:50.348655008-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T20:06:13.576140339-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T20:06:13.576140339-05:00","close_reason":"Added ntm integration to install.sh with detection, prompt, and installation functions"}

-{"id":"bd-5iwb","title":"Implement file size limits and binary detection","description":"# File Size and Binary Detection

## Parent Epic: bd-jk4n (Security Guardrails & Validation)

## Purpose
Prevent committing large files or unexpected binaries.

## File Size Limit

```bash
AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_FILE_MB="${AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_FILE_MB:-10}"

is_file_too_large() {
    local file="$1"
    local max_bytes=$((AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_FILE_MB * 1024 * 1024))
    local size
    
    # Cross-platform stat
    size=$(stat -f%z "$file" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    [[ "$size" -gt "$max_bytes" ]]
}
```

## Binary Detection

```bash
is_binary_file() {
    local file="$1"
    # file command returns "... text" for text files
    \! file -b "$file" 2>/dev/null | grep -qiE "(text|script|json|xml|empty)"
}
```

## Integration

Check during validate_commit_plan():
1. For each file in plan
2. If file exists and is too large: block
3. If file is binary without explicit allow: block

## Configuration

- --max-file-mb=N: Override default 10MB limit
- AGENT_SWEEP_ALLOW_BINARY_PATTERNS: Patterns for allowed binaries

## Allowed Binary Patterns

Some binaries are expected (icons, fonts):
```
*.png
*.jpg
*.gif
*.ico
*.woff
*.woff2
```

## Error Messages

- "File too large: path/to/file (15MB > 10MB limit)"
- "Binary file without explicit allow: path/to/binary"","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:53:39.776937603-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T22:38:58.608873099-05:00"}

 {"id":"bd-5jph","title":"Implement work item priority scoring algorithm","description":"# Task: Implement Work Item Priority Scoring Algorithm

## Purpose
Calculate priority scores for individual issues and PRs (not repos) to enable intelligent work ordering. Higher scores = review first.

## Background: Why Item-Level Scoring?
- Repo-level scoring is too coarse
- A repo with 100 issues shouldn't dominate queue
- Security bugs in small repos should surface
- Enables filtering: --priority=high skips LOW items

## Score Components

### Component 1: Type Importance (0-20 points)
```bash
# PRs indicate someone invested effort
if [[ "$item_type" == "pr" ]]; then
    score=$((score + 20))
    # Draft PRs get penalized
    [[ "$is_draft" == "true" ]] && score=$((score - 15))
else
    score=$((score + 10))
fi
```

### Component 2: Label-Based Priority (0-50 points)
```bash
if echo "$labels" | grep -qiE 'security|critical'; then
    score=$((score + 50))
elif echo "$labels" | grep -qiE 'bug|urgent'; then
    score=$((score + 30))
elif echo "$labels" | grep -qiE 'enhancement|feature'; then
    score=$((score + 10))
fi
```

### Component 3: Age Factor (0-50 points for bugs, penalty for old features)
```bash
age_days=$(days_since_timestamp "$created_at")

# Bug/security items: older = more urgent (they're waiting!)
if echo "$labels" | grep -qiE 'bug|security|critical'; then
    if [[ $age_days -gt 60 ]]; then
        score=$((score + 50))
    elif [[ $age_days -gt 30 ]]; then
        score=$((score + 30))
    elif [[ $age_days -gt 14 ]]; then
        score=$((score + 15))
    fi
else
    # Feature requests: very old ones may be stale, don't prioritize
    if [[ $age_days -gt 180 ]]; then
        score=$((score - 10))
    fi
fi
```

### Component 4: Recency Bonus (0-15 points)
```bash
# Recently updated items have active engagement
updated_days=$(days_since_timestamp "$updated_at")
if [[ $updated_days -lt 3 ]]; then
    score=$((score + 15))
elif [[ $updated_days -lt 7 ]]; then
    score=$((score + 10))
fi
```

### Component 5: Staleness Penalty (-20 points)
```bash
# Already reviewed items should be deprioritized
local item_key="${repo_id}#${item_type}-${number}"
if item_recently_reviewed "$item_key"; then
    score=$((score - 20))
fi
```

## Helper Functions

### days_since_timestamp()
```bash
days_since_timestamp() {
    local ts="$1"
    local now=$(date +%s)
    local then
    # Handle both Linux and macOS date formats
    then=$(date -d "$ts" +%s 2>/dev/null || \
           date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ" "$ts" +%s 2>/dev/null || \
           echo "$now")
    echo $(( (now - then) / 86400 ))
}
```

### item_recently_reviewed()
```bash
item_recently_reviewed() {
    local item_key="$1"
    local state_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/review-state.json"
    
    [[ ! -f "$state_file" ]] && return 1
    
    local last_review
    last_review=$(jq -r --arg key "$item_key" '.items[$key].last_review // ""' "$state_file")
    
    [[ -z "$last_review" ]] && return 1
    
    local days_since
    days_since=$(days_since_timestamp "$last_review")
    
    [[ $days_since -lt ${REVIEW_SKIP_DAYS:-7} ]]
}
```

### get_priority_level()
```bash
get_priority_level() {
    local score="$1"
    if [[ $score -ge 150 ]]; then echo "CRITICAL"
    elif [[ $score -ge 100 ]]; then echo "HIGH"
    elif [[ $score -ge 50 ]]; then echo "NORMAL"
    else echo "LOW"
    fi
}
```

### passes_priority_threshold()
```bash
passes_priority_threshold() {
    local level="$1"
    local threshold="$2"
    
    case "$threshold" in
        all) return 0 ;;
        normal) [[ "$level" != "LOW" ]] ;;
        high) [[ "$level" == "HIGH" || "$level" == "CRITICAL" ]] ;;
        critical) [[ "$level" == "CRITICAL" ]] ;;
    esac
}
```

## Priority Levels

| Score Range | Level | Color | Behavior |
|-------------|-------|-------|----------|
| 150+ | CRITICAL | Red | Process immediately |
| 100-149 | HIGH | Orange | First batch |
| 50-99 | NORMAL | Yellow | When capacity available |
| 0-49 | LOW | Gray | Last, or skip with --skip-low |

## Example Scores
- Security bug, 45 days old: 10 + 50 + 30 = 90 (HIGH)
- New PR with bug label: 20 + 30 + 15 = 65 (NORMAL)
- Draft PR, enhancement: 20 - 15 + 10 = 15 (LOW)
- Critical issue, 90 days: 10 + 50 + 50 = 110 (HIGH)

## Testing
- Unit tests for each component
- Edge cases: no labels, very old items, draft PRs
- Verify staleness penalty applies correctly
- Verify threshold filtering works

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] calculate_item_priority_score() returns consistent scores
- [ ] All 5 components contribute appropriately
- [ ] Priority levels map correctly to score ranges
- [ ] Threshold filtering works for all levels
- [ ] Days calculation works on Linux and macOS","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:18:52.223453659-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T17:02:44.641869392-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T17:02:44.641869392-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented complete priority scoring system: days_since_timestamp(), item_recently_reviewed(), calculate_item_priority_score() with 5 components (type/labels/age/recency/staleness), get_priority_level(), passes_priority_threshold(), score_and_sort_work_items(). All tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5jph","depends_on_id":"bd-ff8h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:52.872108552-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-5ljl","title":"Update CI workflow for agent-sweep tests","description":"# CI Workflow Update for agent-sweep Tests

## Parent Epic: bd-a2wt (Testing Strategy)

## Purpose
Add agent-sweep tests to the CI workflow so tests run automatically on PRs.

## Changes to .github/workflows/ci.yml

### Add New Test Jobs

```yaml
agent-sweep-unit-tests:
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - name: Install test dependencies
      run: |
        sudo apt-get update
        sudo apt-get install -y jq
    - name: Run ntm driver unit tests
      run: scripts/test_unit_ntm_driver.sh
    - name: Run security guardrail tests
      run: scripts/test_security_guardrails.sh
    - name: Run preflight check tests
      run: scripts/test_preflight_checks.sh
    - name: Run plan validation tests
      run: scripts/test_plan_validation.sh
    - name: Run state management tests
      run: scripts/test_state_management.sh

agent-sweep-e2e-tests:
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - name: Install test dependencies
      run: |
        sudo apt-get update
        sudo apt-get install -y jq tmux
    - name: Setup mock ntm
      run: |
        mkdir -p ~/.local/bin
        cp scripts/test_bin/ntm ~/.local/bin/
        chmod +x ~/.local/bin/ntm
        export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"
    - name: Run E2E tests with mock
      run: scripts/test_e2e_agent_sweep.sh
```

### Add Test Matrix
Consider running tests on multiple platforms:
- ubuntu-latest
- macos-latest (if applicable)

### Integration with Existing Jobs
- Run agent-sweep tests in parallel with existing tests
- Use job dependencies to ensure ShellCheck passes first

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All unit test scripts run in CI
- [ ] E2E tests run with mock ntm
- [ ] Tests pass on ubuntu-latest
- [ ] ShellCheck job runs before test jobs
- [ ] Test failures block PR merge
- [ ] Test output is visible in GitHub Actions logs
